J1.basis: This SaaS pivoted from "InboxZap" (email cleanup) to "Lumina" (AI meeting assistant)... the blog's 12 published posts (and all of its historical Google Search Console authority) are about email cleanup/inbox management — entirely off-topic for an AI meeting assistant
J1.rationale: Explicitly names the pivot, identifies live site = meeting assistant vs GSC = legacy inbox demand vs stale repo artifacts, and frames every recommendation (redirects, new cluster, layout reconciliation) around that reality.

J2.basis: `/blog/post-1` → `/blog` — 3900 impressions, 2 clicks, avg pos 42.3 ... `/blog/post-7` → `/blog` — 80 impressions, 0 clicks, avg pos 55.8
J2.rationale: Walks all 12 legacy posts individually with real GSC impressions/clicks/positions and assigns an explicit disposition with reasoning; docked because every asset lands on the identical blanket disposition (301 to hub) with no differentiation between the 3,900-impression post and the 80-impression one, and no harvest/keep considered.

J3.basis: the closest relevant page is the blog hub itself, so all 12 redirect there rather than sunset — this preserves crawl paths and any residual link equity instead of turning indexed URLs into 404s... build the meeting-assistant cluster as the growth engine
J3.rationale: Explicitly names the tension and avoids both failure modes: preserves legacy equity via 301s (not 404 torching) while building the new meeting-productivity cluster as the growth engine and sequencing the transition in the roadmap.
